title: PROJECT.yaml v1
description: Tree-resident manifest for a Runko Project (design.md §7.2, §10.1-10.4, §22.3 #1). This is the L0/L1 core: only 'schema', 'name', and 'type' are required. Everything else is optional and either inherited (owners), inferred (dependencies), or opt-in (capabilities, ci, visibility) per the layering rule in §6.2 - L2/L3 fields must never be required for a default project. Validated against the YAML file's JSON-equivalent parse tree. Canonical project ID, created_at, template version, effective owners, and inferred dependency edges are control-plane-derived (§10.2) and deliberately absent from this on-disk schema.

schema.description: Schema version tag. Bump the literal ('project/v2', ...) on any breaking change; readers must reject unknown values rather than guess.

name.description: Project identifier, unique within the monorepo. DNS-label-like: lowercase, digits, hyphens. This is the name used in owners rules, dependency edges, affected-project output, and CLI/MCP references.

type.description: Project intent, per §7.2. 'library': shared code, no deploy surface by default. 'service': long-running backend (deploy capability optional). 'app': user-facing application. 'job': batch/cron worker. 'other': escape hatch for anything that doesn't fit.

language.description: L0, optional. Primary language recorded at create time ('go', 'python', 'ts', 'rust', 'java', 'cpp', ...). Deliberately a pattern, not an enum: no-template creates (§10.4) record any language verbatim ('haskell', 'c++'). Absent = unspecified (create-time default templates are Go); languages actually used are inferred continuously by the indexer regardless (§10.2).
language.$comment: Echoed verbatim from the create intent, never default-filled - a defaulted (Go) create leaves this key absent.

owners.description: L0 field, optional. Explicit owner refs (e.g. 'group:commerce-eng', 'user:jdoe'). Omitted -> effective owners are inherited from the nearest ancestor OWNERS file, or the org's create-time default (creator's primary group) per §7.3, §10.1. Presence here overrides inherited OWNERS for this project's root.

capabilities.description: L2, opt-in. Enabling a capability is a product action (`project add-capability <cap>`) that triggers template codegen; hand-editing this array without going through that flow may leave generated files inconsistent (§7.2, §10.4). Absence = no runtime surface beyond code. 'build' declares a build-graph binding (engine + target patterns, see capability_config) enabling adapter-refined affected computation (§14.5.4); it is never required for a default project (NG4).

capability_config.description: L2/L3, optional. Org-defined configuration keyed by capability name (e.g. { "deploy": {...}, "rpc": {...} }). Intentionally loosely typed here: deploy/rpc/http/data_store config shapes are org-specific templates (§7.2) and out of scope for this core schema. Keys should correspond to entries in `capabilities`. The 'build' key is the one documented exception - see docs/spec/build-adapter/README.md for its { engine: 'bazel'|'buck2', target_patterns: [string] } shape.

dependencies.description: L2/L3, optional, rare. Explicitly DECLARED dependency edges (other project names) that participate in affected-computation gating (§13.3). Most dependency data should come from the async inferred-dependency indexer, which is advisory-only and never written here; promoting an inferred edge to declared is a deliberate action, not automatic sync.

root_invalidation.description: L3, root-manifest-oriented. Glob patterns (path.Match plus 'prefix/**' subtrees) naming BUILD-SENSITIVE paths whose change invalidates every project: toolchain manifests (go.mod, MODULE.bazel), build entrypoints (Makefile, Dockerfile), CI definitions (.github/**). A matching path escalates affected computation to run_everything (§14.5.2). Tree-borne per §9.4 ('the tree owns policy' - relocated from the daemon's --root-invalidation flag, which remains as an additive override): the platform and runko-ci read these from the indexed manifests, so policy rides the same review gates as the code it protects.

prose.description: L3, root-manifest-oriented; ORDERED, first match wins. The de-escalation dual of root_invalidation (§14.5.7): a changed path matching a prose pattern is re-attributed, for check derivation only, to the repo-root project - it requires the root project's content-tier checks and feeds no dependency closure. Same glob dialect as root_invalidation plus a leading '**/' any-depth form and a gitignore-style '!' prefix for exceptions (a '!' match means NOT prose). root_invalidation always wins over prose; the §7.3 owner gate is untouched (it derives from raw touched paths); a repo with no root project gets no de-escalation. Paths tests consume as data must be '!'-excepted and covered by a declared check.

visibility.description: L3, rare. 'restricted' opts this project into per-principal read enforcement (Josh-filtered remotes, search filtering, orientation redaction) per §15.2. Hard limit: this is access control over a shared object store, not isolation (NG7) - documented, not hidden.

ci.description: L2, optional. Low-ceremony CI hints per §14.9; the default template for `type` already supplies sane checks (e.g. go-service -> unit + lint) so most projects never edit this.

name.description: Check name; maps to a required Check on Changes touching this project (§14.4.2, §14.9).

command.description: Template-defined by default; override only to deviate from the golden path.
